item.title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-09 - Today's Summary
item.description: Developer attention today is split between two strong currents: AI agent frameworks and local LLM inference tooling. The sustained hot streak of 'pm-skills' (13k+ stars, +798 in 24h) and the acceleration of 'whichllm' (+630 in 24h) suggest builders are actively seeking structured agentic capabilities and hardware-aware local model selection. Meanwhile, 'AiToEarn' (+873 in 24h) broke out suddenly, indicating a new wave of interest in AI-powered content monetization platforms. Computer vision tools like 'supervision' and 'opencv' remain steady but not accelerating. On Hacker News, a thread titled 'LLMs are eroding my software engineering career' garnered 1119 votes, reflecting developer anxiety about AI's impact on jobs—a sentiment that may be fueling interest in agent frameworks that augment rather than replace. The absence of major launches on Product Hunt today suggests the attention is concentrated on GitHub projects rather than new products. Top projects: Andyyyy64/whichllm, phuryn/pm-skills, roboflow/supervision, opencv/opencv, yikart/AiToEarn, Tencent/ncnn, ChatGPTNextWeb/NextChat, hpcaitech/ColossalAI, google-ai-edge/mediapipe, onlook-dev/onlook, NirDiamant/RAG_Techniques, automazeio/ccpm Note: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。 Note: daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。 Note: Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。 Note: Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

item.title: [sustained_hot] Andyyyy64/whichllm (+630 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Local LLM / Inference Description: A CLI tool that auto-detects your hardware and ranks local LLMs by real, recency-aware benchmarks — not just parameter count. Why it matters: With 3,800 stars and a 24-hour delta of +630, whichllm is experiencing a sustained surge in attention. The project has a recent release (within 30 days) and active commits within the last 7 days, indicating ongoing development and community engagement. The acceleration ratio is low (1.0), suggesting steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward tooling that abstracts away the complexity of local LLM deployment. Developers should expect more 'auto-pilot' tools that handle hardware detection, model selection, and optimization, reducing friction for end users and enabling broader adoption of local AI. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] phuryn/pm-skills (+798 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: PM Skills Marketplace is an open-source collection of 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins for AI assistants, turning generic AI into a structured product management co-pilot. Why it matters: The repo has 13,158 stars and gained 798 stars in the last 24 hours, indicating strong and sustained attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), meaning growth is steady rather than explosive, but the consistent high velocity suggests deep community interest. This is not a flash-in-the-pan; it's a project with staying power. Developer insight: The project signals a shift from general-purpose AI to specialized, framework-driven agents. For AI developers, this means investing in domain-specific skill repositories could be a high-impact strategy. The plugin architecture is portable across assistants, reducing lock-in risk. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] roboflow/supervision (+728 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Computer Vision / Developer Tools Description: A reusable, model-agnostic Python toolkit for computer vision tasks like annotation, tracking, and dataset management. Why it matters: With 42,680 stars and a 24-hour delta of +728, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic interest rather than a sudden spike. It's actively maintained (commits within 7 days) and has a large community (3,811 forks, 104 open issues). The consistent daily star gain suggests it's a go-to resource for CV practitioners. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward modular, composable CV toolkits. Developers increasingly expect plug-and-play components that work across models and frameworks. Supervision's success indicates that the community values interoperability and ease of use over monolithic solutions. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] opencv/opencv (+322 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Computer Vision / Deep Learning Infrastructure Description: The de facto open-source library for computer vision and image processing, now integrating deep learning capabilities. Why it matters: With 88,427 stars and a sustained 222-star daily delta over the past three days, OpenCV continues to attract massive attention. The acceleration ratio of 1 indicates steady, organic growth rather than a sudden spike. This consistent velocity reflects its essential role in AI and robotics workflows. Developer insight: For AI developers, OpenCV signals the maturation of computer vision as a commodity capability. The library's sustained popularity indicates that vision is becoming a standard component in AI systems, not a niche specialty. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] yikart/AiToEarn (+873 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Other AI Description: An open-source AI agent platform for content marketing and monetization across 14+ social platforms, targeting solo entrepreneurs and creators. Why it matters: The repo gained 873 stars in the last 24 hours (309/day average) with a sudden breakout pattern, reaching 19,663 total stars. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), but the rule-based trend type is 'sudden_breakout', indicating a sharp increase in attention. This is likely driven by the recent v2.4 release (May 21, 2026) adding new AI models and enhanced batch generation, combined with growing interest in AI-powered content automation tools. Developer insight: The rise of AI-powered content marketing agents signals a shift toward autonomous content operations. Developers should explore MCP protocol integration and agent-based automation for social media management, as this could become a standard workflow for creator tools. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] Tencent/ncnn (+10 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Local LLM / Inference Description: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for mobile, embedded, and desktop deployment, with no third-party runtime dependencies and support for CPU and Vulkan GPU backends. Why it matters: The project shows a sudden breakout with a 24-hour star delta of 8 and a 7-day delta of 36, but more importantly, the acceleration ratio is 2.4 (high), indicating a sharp increase in attention velocity. With 23,347 total stars and a recent release within 30 days, the project is experiencing a clear spike in interest, likely driven by new capabilities or community events. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward more efficient, platform-agnostic inference frameworks that can run on diverse hardware (CPU, Vulkan, RISC-V). Developers should consider ncnn for projects requiring low-latency inference on mobile or embedded devices, especially if they already use PyTorch. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] ChatGPTNextWeb/NextChat (+18 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Local LLM / Inference Description: NextChat is a lightweight, cross-platform AI chat client that supports multiple LLMs (Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, DeepSeek) with one-click deployment and a privacy-first local-storage design. Why it matters: With 88,131 stars and a 24h star delta of 22, the project shows sustained high interest. The acceleration ratio of 2.9 (high) indicates a sudden breakout in attention, likely driven by recent feature additions like MCP support, real-time chat, and the iOS app release. The 7-day average star delta of 7.8 confirms consistent growth, but the acceleration signals a recent spike. Developer insight: The rapid adoption of NextChat signals a shift toward unified, self-hosted AI interfaces that reduce dependency on single providers. The MCP support hints at a future where AI tools interoperate via standardized protocols, opening opportunities for plugin ecosystems. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] hpcaitech/ColossalAI (+7 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Distributed Training & Inference Infrastructure Description: Colossal-AI is an open-source deep learning system that makes large AI models cheaper, faster, and more accessible through advanced parallelism and heterogeneous memory management. Why it matters: The repo has a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 6 (high), despite a low 7-day average star delta of -0.6. With 41,385 total stars and 4,505 forks, it's a well-established project that recently saw a spike in attention, possibly due to new benchmarks (B200 GPUs showing 50-70% throughput gains) or the release of Open-Sora and DeepSeek fine-tuning guides. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward democratizing large model training: developers can now achieve near-linear scaling on multi-GPU setups with minimal code changes, reducing the barrier to entry for building foundation models. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] google-ai-edge/mediapipe (+30 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Local LLM / Inference Description: Google's cross-platform framework for building on-device ML pipelines for live and streaming media. Why it matters: With 35,541 stars and a sudden breakout acceleration of 3.1 (high), the project gained 30 stars in the last 24 hours and 104 in the last week. This sudden acceleration suggests a spike in attention, possibly due to new capabilities or community momentum, making it a hot topic for on-device ML deployment. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward on-device AI as a standard expectation, with MediaPipe lowering the barrier for developers to add real-time perception features without deep ML expertise. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] onlook-dev/onlook (+9 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: An open-source, AI-first visual editor for building and editing React (Next.js + TailwindCSS) apps directly in the browser, positioning itself as the 'Cursor for Designers.' Why it matters: With 25,896 stars, a 24-hour delta of +7, and a high acceleration of 2.3 (sudden breakout), Onlook is experiencing a sharp spike in attention. The star velocity has jumped from a 7-day average of 5.2 to a 3-day average of 3, but the acceleration metric indicates a recent surge beyond organic growth. This suggests a viral moment or external catalyst, making it a hot project to watch. Developer insight: Signals a shift toward AI-first design tools that treat code as the source of truth. For AI developers, this validates the market for agentic coding assistants that integrate with visual editors, especially for React ecosystems. Risk: Medium

item.title: [sudden_breakout] NirDiamant/RAG_Techniques (+34 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: RAG / Knowledge Base Description: This repository showcases various advanced techniques for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. Each technique has a detailed notebook tutorial. Why it matters: RAG / Knowledge Base 方向出现突然加速：24h stars +34，前 3 日均值 13.3，加速度 2.6x，今天值得优先排查触发原因。 Developer insight: 重点看数据接入、检索质量和 production workflow，而不是只看 demo 效果。 Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] automazeio/ccpm (+5 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: Project management skill system for Agents that uses GitHub Issues and Git worktrees for parallel agent execution. Why it matters: AI Agent Framework 方向出现突然加速：24h stars +5，前 3 日均值 2，加速度 2.5x，今天值得优先排查触发原因。 Developer insight: 信号存在不确定性，建议先检查 README、最近 commit 和 issue 质量后再投入时间。 Risk: Medium
